task_signature.context: Which semantic tokens implement which UX foundations principles. Used in Phase 3 compliance gateway checks.

learned_patterns.decision: Use AppSize.ctaHeight (52pt) + AppCornerRadius.large (20pt) + frame(maxWidth: .infinity). Full-row buttons in TrainingPlanView use AppSize.rowMinHeight (44pt). Tab bar targets: 44pt minimum per iOS HIG.
Home screen surfaces ONE primary action via AppPrimaryButton. Onboarding: exactly 4 goal options. Never >6 items in a flat list without grouping.
source_executions: home-today-screen v2 decisions: HOME ONE primary CTA

learned_patterns.pattern: Visual hierarchy — color tokens by semantic role
learned_patterns.decision: AppColor.Text.primary for body copy, AppColor.Text.secondary for captions/labels, AppColor.Text.tertiary for metadata (WCAG AA: ~4.6:1 light, ~5.1:1 dark). Never raw Color.gray or hex.
source_executions: home-today-screen v2 F7 (text hierarchy violations found in v1)

learned_patterns.pattern: Surface layering — depth via surface tokens
learned_patterns.decision: AppColor.Background.appPrimary (base canvas) → AppColor.Surface.primary (cards) → AppColor.Surface.elevated (modal/sheet tops) → AppColor.Surface.materialStrong (blur overlays). Never mix raw Color.white or Color.black.
source_executions: home-today-screen v2 F3 (gradient background decision: appBlue1→appBlue2)

learned_patterns.decision: AppColor.Status.success / .warning / .error for all system feedback states. Never raw Color.green, Color.red, Color.yellow. Used in empty states, error banners, metric readiness indicators.
source_executions: home-today-screen v2 F15 (empty state needed status signaling)

learned_patterns.pattern: Data visualization — chart tokens for metric tints
learned_patterns.decision: AppColor.Chart.* for all metric visualizations. Specific: .weight, .hrv, .heartRate, .activity (added in Home v2 T3). Also: .body, .cardio, .sleep, .achievement, .progress, .nutritionFat. Prevents competing tints.

token_mapping.anti_token: Color(hex: "#FF6B35") or arbitrary tint per metric

source_executions: home-today-screen v2 F8 (chart token gap → T3 added chart tokens)

learned_patterns.pattern: Brand accent — primary vs accent distinction
learned_patterns.decision: AppColor.Brand.primary for main brand identity. AppColor.Accent.primary (= Brand.primary) for interactive CTAs. AppColor.Accent.recovery, .sleep, .achievement for domain-specific accents. AppColor.Brand.warmSoft / .coolSoft for soft tints on cards.

token_mapping.anti_token: Color("appBlue1") directly in views — always go through AppColor.Brand.*

source_executions: home-today-screen v2 F5 (AppText tokens), F8 (Chart tokens)

learned_patterns.decision: AppColor.Border.strong for prominent dividers, .subtle for card edges, .hairline for 0.5pt pixel-level separators. Never .overlay(Rectangle().frame(height: 1).foregroundStyle(.gray)).

token_mapping.anti_token: Divider() with default color or Rectangle().fill(Color.gray.opacity(0.3))

learned_patterns.decision: AppColor.Background.authTop / .authMiddle / .authBottom are always dark (same value in light and dark mode). Auth screens never use system adaptive backgrounds. This is intentional brand decision.

token_mapping.anti_token: Color.black or Color("darkBlue") in auth views

anti_patterns.pattern: Raw color literal in view file
anti_patterns.what_went_wrong: Home v1 used Color(red:green:blue:) for metric tints. Broke dark mode, failed tokens-check CI gate, required full audit in v2 pass. 8 P0 findings traced to raw literals.
Bypasses the type scale. Dynamic Type scaling breaks. Audit finding F5 in home-today-screen v2. tokens-check CI gate catches this.
Inconsistent with AppSpacing tokens. Fails make tokens-check. Should be AppSpacing.cardPadding or AppSpacing.screenHorizontal.

speedup_instructions: When running Phase 3 compliance gateway, use this file to verify every color, typography, and spacing token against the principle it implements. The token_mapping fields in each learned_pattern tell you exactly which tokens satisfy each principle. Anti-patterns tell you what CI will catch — fix those before running make tokens-check.
